supporting_evidence: QQQ gained only +0.62% today while SMH surged +2.97% and NVDA rose +4.20%, shattering the software-outpacing-hardware divergence signal that anchors the thesis.

Trading volume is just 0.73x the 30-day average at 32.6M shares, indicating a lack of institutional conviction behind this marginal green close while price remains below the $712.57 10-day MA and near the 0.16 position in its recent range.

China’s explicit $295B AI strategy to exclude foreign firms, combined with Singapore’s seizure of Nvidia chips in an export control probe, signals tightening regulatory and geopolitical headwinds that will compress QQQ’s mega-cap revenue streams.
key_assumption: The bull assumes hyperscaler AI CapEx will rapidly translate into software/platform earnings growth for QQQ constituents; this is fragile because capital allocation is currently bottlenecked at the hardware procurement stage, while geopolitical decoupling and export controls are actively shrinking the addressable market for US cloud and software providers.
regime_sensitivity: Weakens significantly in a high_vix crisis regime due to duration risk, geopolitical contagion, and rate sensitivity, but even in low_vix it lacks conviction volume and relative strength to justify accumulation or hold positioning.
watch_items: QQQ breaks below $706.52 (10-day low) on a down day with volume exceeding 35M shares
SMH maintains a >3% outperformance spread over QQQ for three consecutive trading sessions
